questions.question: Justify that arithmetic progressions represent linear growth.
questions.answer: Arithmetic progressions have a constant difference between terms, which corresponds to linear growth.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions cannot model exponential growth.
questions.answer: Exponential growth involves multiplication, while APs involve addition.

questions.question: Explain why nth term formula is essential in arithmetic progressions.
questions.answer: It allows direct computation of any term without listing previous terms.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are used in modelling uniform motion.
questions.answer: Uniform motion involves equal changes over equal intervals, matching AP structure.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are preferred in simple forecasting.
questions.answer: They provide straightforward predictions based on constant change.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are fundamental to series concepts.
questions.answer: They form the basis for arithmetic series and summation formulas.

questions.question: Explain why common difference determines the entire arithmetic progression.
questions.answer: Once the first term and common difference are fixed, all terms are determined.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are suitable for salary increment models.
questions.answer: Salary increments often increase by a fixed amount each year.

questions.question: Explain why negative arithmetic progressions represent depreciation.
questions.answer: Negative common difference models uniform decrease in value.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are used in cost estimation.
questions.answer: Costs increasing uniformly over time can be modelled using APs.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions help develop algebraic thinking.
questions.answer: They involve formulas, variables and pattern generalisation.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are ideal for modelling linear trends.
questions.answer: Linear trends involve constant change, matching AP structure.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are insufficient for compound interest.
questions.answer: Compound interest grows multiplicatively, not additively.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are foundational in sequences and series.
questions.answer: They introduce systematic growth patterns essential for advanced series.

questions.question: Explain the role of arithmetic progressions in mathematical modelling.
questions.answer: They model situations involving uniform increase or decrease.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are widely used in education.
questions.answer: They are simple yet powerful tools for understanding sequences.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions improve logical reasoning skills.
questions.answer: They require identifying patterns and applying formulas logically.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions remain relevant in modern mathematics.
questions.answer: They are used in modelling, forecasting and mathematical analysis.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are used in economics.
questions.answer: They model uniform growth or decline in economic quantities.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are useful in planning schedules.
questions.answer: Uniform increments in time or effort form arithmetic progressions.

questions.question: Explain the limitations of arithmetic progressions in real-life modelling.
questions.answer: They cannot accurately represent non-linear or exponential changes.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are preferred in simple trend analysis.
questions.answer: Because they are easy to interpret and apply.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions help in systematic problem solving.
questions.answer: They provide structured formulas and clear patterns.

questions.question: Explain why arithmetic progressions are essential before learning series.
questions.answer: Understanding APs is necessary before studying arithmetic series.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of arithmetic progressions in mathematics.
questions.answer: Arithmetic progressions play a crucial role in sequences, series, modelling and real-life applications.
